gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/364)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/364)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الذَّهَبِ يُحَلَّى بِهِ الصِّبْيَانُ فَقَالَ إِنَّهُ كَانَ أَبِي ( عليه السلام ) لَيُحَلِّي وُلْدَهُ وَ نِسَاءَهُ بِالذَّهَبِ وَ الْفِضَّةِ فَلَا بَأْسَ بِهِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Washa and Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, altogether from Dawood Bin Sirham who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the gold the children are ornamented by, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘My<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup> used to ornament his<sup>asws</sup> children and his<sup>asws</sup> wives with the gold and the silver, so there is no problem with it’.
en.sarwar: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Washsha’ and Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr all from Dawud ibn Sarhan who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of children; if they can use gold as ornaments. He (the Imam) said, ‘My father, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, allowed his children and women to use gold and silver as ornaments, so it is not unlawful.’”
